entry.title: Syniti RDG adds a feature of Importing Workflow from one change request to another change request.
entry.content:
Syniti Rapid Data Governance (RDG)  is a Cloud-Based SAP UI5 application built on the SAP Business Technology Platform, simplifying the process of SAP Master Data Governance. This Product helps the user to create workflows based on the Change Request as per the business needs using its Manage Workflow tile straightforwardly with minimal technical skills.  

Syniti RDG automates the processes of MDG Data Model Maintenance, Business Rules, Field Property Configuration, Interface Configuration, and Master Data Consolidation with an intuitive, user-friendly, and elegant graphical interface. 

With the series of different blogs, we have already come across the various processes of RDG and how it works with different functionalities. Henceforth this blog is contributed to show an extended feature of RDG that is importing workflow from one Change Request to another. This import functionality is more efficient and user-friendly by automating the BADI implementation and BRF+ steps. 

Below are the steps to import a Workflow from one Change Request to another without copying the existing whole Change Request Properties using Syniti RDG: 

  • Open the RDG UI and click 'Manage MDG Workflow' from the home screen. 
  • Click the  

     

    icon to create a new change request.
  • Enter the required details and Save: 
  • CR Type 
  • Description 
  • Data Model 
  • Main Entity 
  • Business Activity 

Leading Entities will default from the Main Entity. 

  • Click on Save.  
  • Select transport request. 
  • A successful message should appear. Select  

     

    and CR type got created. 
  • Select the Import Workflow button once the new CR Type is created. 
  • Select the Data Model and reference the CR Type from which the workflow will be imported. Click on OK 
  • Select a Transport Request. 
  • A successful message should appear. Select 

     

     and Workflow got imported.

     

     

 

 

Syniti RDG is a certified SAP BTP solution on the SAP App Store. 

 To know more details about the product, check the blog Partner Add-on Solutions. 

Your opinions and feedback are highly appreciated, and feel free to post them in the comment section. 

Please follow and read more interesting blogs on SAP Master Data Governance | SAP | SAP Blogs 

You can also find and post questions about the product here: SAP Master Data Governance Community 

 

 Please also check out more details about the product from other blogs:

  • Partner Add-on Solutions

entry.title: Concento-RDG provides an effortless parallel and dynamic workflow maintenance.
entry.content:
SAP Master Data Governance is a powerful solution to improve the quality and consistency of information across organisations by consolidating and centrally governing master data. Syniti Rapid Data Governance (RDG) is a SAP Certified accelerator that automates multiple MDG tasks such as Data Model Maintenance, Workflow Design and Business Rules in an intuitive, elegant, and graphical interface so that even users with no technical experience can use it. 

Depending on business requirements, Syniti RDG will allow users to implement Dynamic and Parallel steps for complex workflow scenarios automating the tedious tasks of workflow maintenance in a traditional approach. 

Typically, a user would follow the SAP provided guide to customize the workflow traditionally for a parallel scenario. Link to the document: 

Set up Parallel Workflow Tasks with BRF+ (BADI USMD_SSW_PARA_RESULT_HANDLER) 

These steps include: 

  • Creating a new change request type. 
  • Defining service names 
  • Populating BRF+ Single, User and Non-User Agent tables. 
  • Create Enhancement implementation for BAdIs to customize Parallel and Dynamic tasks. 
  • USMD_SSW_SERVICE_PROCESSOR 
  • USMD_SSW_SYSTEM_METHOD_CALLER 
  • USMD_SSW_DYNAMIC_AGENT_SELECT 
  • Create filter and set service names for BAdIs as conditions. 

Some of the standard screens to customize parallel and dynamic workflow included in above guide: 

 

 

With RDG, user can easily handle the steps included in guide having no prior experience in BRF+ application, Decision Tables and ABAP programming to implement BAdIs as RDG automates the entire implementation of BAdIs, and BRF+ tables to handle Dynamic and Parallel workflow tasks in single user friendly and intuitive UI Screen. 

Steps to set up initial workflow in RDG is mentioned in the blog here: 

RDG Simplifies Rule-Based Workflow Maintenance 

Below are the additional steps to perform in RDG for Dynamic and Parallel workflow tasks: 

  • The user can now assign approvers and create Dynamic and Parallel Flow steps according to the requirements for workflow selecting from the list. 
  • For a Parallel workflow Scenario, when all company codes are to be approved by its corresponding owners, we choose company code as attribute from the entity BP_CUS_CC entity and give list of company codes for which the approvals are necessary. Workflow requests will be sent to all the approvers parallelly. 

Select Parallel Flow from the list. 

  • Click Add Attribute to choose entity and attribute.

     

  • Click on Add Rule to add a row for details. Enter the Company Codes and Approval steps.            
  •  Similarly, in Dynamic scenario the request is routed dynamically based on the values of sales organisation, distribution channel. Select Dynamic Rules from the list. Choose entities and attributes required.
  • On save of the workflow, BRF decision tables are created and populated automatically for rule based parallel and dynamic workflow. 

Decision tables populated for Rule-Based Workflow.

Service Names as filter conditions. 

Thus, a Rule Based Workflow can be maintained even for complex scenarios in RDG with no prior knowledge in ABAP and BRF+. 

 

 

 

 

 

Syniti RDG is available on SAP store.  Please also check out more details about the product from the blog about Partner Add-on Solutions.

entry.title: Single Value Validation and Derivation Rules Using Syniti RDG
entry.content:
Syniti RDG is an SAP-endorsed application on the Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to facilitate MDG implementation with intuitive UI and process automation. RDG automates many of the technical configuration steps that are required to implement SAP MDG and it offers a user-friendly interface to be more relatable to business users.  

The below example is based on the aforementioned guide to create a validation and derivation rule to be triggered for a specific Change Request Type for attribute values in Entity AD_POSTAL of Data Model BP.

Step 1: Create the Validation/Derivation Rule in Syniti RDG 

  • In the main menu, select "Manage Business Rules". 
  • In the following steps, the user can choose a Change request type from the list and select the entity for which the rule has to be created. The user then needs to select the type of Business Rule that he/she wants to create. Value Check Rule to create Validation Rule in BRFPlus and Derivation Rule to create Derivation Rule in BRFPlus.
  • The user then selects the attribute value for which the rule has to be created and provides the condition and action. The user can subsequently specify for which processing step in the workflow should this rule be triggered. On saving, the rule gets created in the corresponding catalog in BRFPlus. 
  • Validation Rule: If AD_POSTAL-REF_POSTA = “CN” or AD_POSTAL = “IN”, then display error message “COUNTRY IS INVALID” for all workflow steps.

Derivation Rule: If AD_POSTAL-REF-POSTA = “US” and AD_POSTAL-CITY1 = “DENVER”, then derive the attribute value AD_POSTAL-POST-COD1 = “80014” only for Initial processing step. Step 2: Trigger the rule in nwbc application. 

  • The rules created in the previous step are triggered when creating a Change Request of the type for which the rules are created in RDG (ZBP1P5).
  • Validation Rule 
  • Derivation RuleBelow are the rules that got automatically generated in BRFPlus under the catalog FMDM_MODEL_BP through Syniti RDG.
  • Validation Rule:

ConditionAction 

 

 

  • Derivation Rule: 

Condition Action 

 With the help of Syniti RDG, you can now create rules without the hassles of attending to the many technical configuration steps of defining these rules in BRFPlus.

 

 

 

 

 

Syniti RDG is a certified SAP BTP solution and is available on SAP App Store. 

 To know more details about the product, check the blog Partner Add-on Solutions. 

Your opinions and feedback are highly appreciated and feel free to post them in the comment section. 

Please follow and read more interesting blogs on SAP Master Data Governance | SAP | SAP Blogs 

You can also find and post questions about the product here: SAP Master Data Governance Community

entry.title: Syniti RDG provides a new functionality on Cross Entity Rule
entry.content:
Syniti RDG is an SAP-endorsed application on the Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to facilitate MDG implementation with intuitive UI and process automation. RDG automates many of the technical configuration steps that are required to implement SAP MDG and it offers a user-friendly interface to be more relatable to business users. 

Typically, a user must perform the steps described to create the Cross Entity Derivation. 

Badi for cross entities, usmd_rule_service_cross_et.  

  1. In SE18, enter the Badi name and display it.  
  2. Right-click and create Badi implementation. 
  3. Enter enhancement class. 
  4. The screen allows you to view and edit the implementation.

 Concento RDG provides a powerful User Interface that helps a business user create multiple value derivation cross-entity rules. 

Step 1: Create the Cross Entity Derivation Rule in Concento RDG.

  • In the main menu, select “Manage Business Rules.” 
  • In the following steps, the user can select a Change Request Type from the list, select the dropdown next to the entity (The entity that contains the attribute you are deriving), and select the Multivalue Derivation. 
  • Add the Deriving Attribute

     

  • Add the Driving Attributes  
  • Driving Attribute – The attribute to be matched against a value or check to determine the property of the Deriving Attribute.  
  • Deriving Attribute – The attribute which changes based on the Driving Attribute. 
  • Select Cross Entity Derivation

     

    and determine whether or not dependent key entries are needed.  
  • Select Add Rule .

     

     
  • Highlight the new line to enter the rule data. 
  • Enter Rule Details for: 
  1. Driving Attributes 
  2. Workflow Step 
  3. Deriving Attribute 
  • Multi-Value Derivation Rule: If BP_HEADER-BU_GROUP = “001”, derive the attribute value BP_ROLE-BP_ROL_ID = FLCU00 and FLCU01 for every workflow step. 
  • You will be prompted with the Transport Request List when you click on Save. Select your TR. Followed by selecting a Package.   
  • The Rule will be created and displayed under the Multivalue Derivation Rule. A successful message will appear. 

The Badi Enhancement is generated in the appropriate Badi. Step 2: Trigger the Rule in the NWBC application.

The rules created in the previous step are triggered when creating a Change Request of the type for which the rules were created in the RDG application. The above example shows how the Concento RDG provides a user-friendly interface to create cross-entity derivatives. 

 

 

Syniti RDG is a certified SAP BTP solution and is available on SAP App Store. 

 To know more details about the product, check the blog Partner Add-on Solutions. 

Your opinions and feedback are highly appreciated and feel free to post them in comment section. 

Please follow and read more interesting blogs on SAP Master Data Governance | SAP | SAP Blogs 

You can also find and post questions about the product here: SAP Master Data Governance Community

entry.title: Syniti RDG provides a new functionality on Four EYE Principle
entry.content:
Syniti RDG is an SAP-endorsed application on the Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to facilitate MDG implementation with intuitive UI and process automation. RDG automates many of the technical configuration steps that are required to implement SAP MDG and it offers a user-friendly interface to be more relatable to business users. 

The Four-Eye Principle can be applied during workflow approval to ensure data quality and governance. When the feature is enabled, the person who initiated the workflow is among the resolved approvers for a particular step of that workflow; they'll be excluded from the list of approvers. 

Concento RDG provides a powerful User Interface that helps a business user enable the Four Eye Principle for workflow approvals. 

STEP 1: Create a CR Type in the RDG Application 

  • From the home screen, select the ‘Manage MDG Workflow’ tile and then click the  

     

    icon. 
  • Enter the required details and select

     


    1. CR Type 
    2. Description 
    3. Data Model 
    4. Edition Type (for FI data models) 
    5. Business Activity 
    6. Choose either a Single Object or a Parallel 
    7. Main Entity 
  • The four eyes principle prevents a requestor from processing or self-approving a change request that they have submitted. It works by removing the requestor from the set of possible authorized users. The requestor is also removed from the group of authorized users in 
    1. A Role 
    2. An Organizational Unit 
    3. A Job 
    4. A Position 
  • Four eyes principle can be applied to any change request type if selected at the time the change request type is created by selecting the checkbox. If the checkbox for the four eyes principle is selected, the option to disable field properties for this change request type is offered. 
  • The workflow graph can be completed using any configuration of approvers and approver types, including parallel and dynamic nodes. 
  • If the four eyes principle is enabled, there will be an Error message in the change request that the requestor cannot approve the change request. Any Save/Approve/Agree buttons will be disabled for the requestor.  

Trigger the Four EYE Principle in the NWBC application.

  • The Four EYE Principle in the previous step is triggered when creating a Change Request of that Change Request type for which the Change Request Type was built in the RDG application.  
  • If Disable Field Properties has not been selected, the Requestor can edit the change request if that option is available at that step of the change request process. 
  • If Disable Field Properties has been selected, the Requestor will not be able to edit the change request even if that option is available at that step of the change request process for other users. 

The above example shows how the Concento RDG provides a user-friendly interface to enable the Four Eye Principle. 

 

 

 

Syniti RDG is a certified SAP BTP solution and is available on SAP App Store. 

 To know more details about the product, check the blog Partner Add-on Solutions. 

Your opinions and feedback are highly appreciated and feel free to post them in comment section. 

Please follow and read more interesting blogs on SAP Master Data Governance | SAP | SAP Blogs 

You can also find and post questions about the product here: SAP Master Data Governance Community

entry.title: Extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using SAPUI5 adaptation projects
entry.content:
You can now work with SAPUI5 adaptation projects to extend your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system by creating application variants that fulfill the specific business needs of different business roles, groups or users, organizational units, and countries. For example, you might want to create an application variant to simplify and streamline a specific process by hiding fields not required by casual users, visualizing data as a chart, or changing a responsive table to a grid table to make information easier to process.

In an adaptation project, developers efficiently use the original SAP Fiori application together with its OData service and application logic to define changes that are specific to the application variant only. After deployment, both the source application and the newly created application variant exist with distinct titles and can be made available to the relevant business users through uniquely named application tiles in My Home, the new starting point for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Fig 1: Create new SAPUI5 adaptation project in SAP Business Application Studio

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Fig 2: Relationship between source application (blue) and application variant created by SAPUI5 Adaptation Project (green).

Cloud upgrade safe

To comply with automated Cloud upgrades and ensure the stability of your extensions, application variants created with SAPUI5 adaptation projects contain a runnable snapshot of the original (base) application containing all UI artifacts and a local copy of all UI annotations. In other words, SAPUI5 flexibility safely separates your extension code from the original application lifecycle, provided you follow the upgrade safe compatibility rules and best practices for controller extensions.  

After a Cloud upgrade, developers can test the application variant in SAP Business Application Studio and, if there are no issues, they can deploy it again to benefit from the latest SAP enhancements to the original application. For more information, see Check Whether the Base App of an Adaptation Project Is Up-To-Date.


Get started

Learn how to create an app variant via SAPUI5 adaptation project by completing these step-by-step developer tutorials https://developers.sap.com/group.sapui5-adaptation-projects.html.

For more information, see: Extending an SAP Fiori Application for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  

To check whether an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition application supports extensibility via SAPUI5 adaptation projects, look up the app in SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library and refer to the Implementation Information under Extensibility (Extensibility via SAPUI5 Adaptation Project: Supported) for example Manage Customer Contracts.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Fig 3: SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library: Implementation Information

If the SAP-delivered application you want to extend with an application variant is not yet supported, please let us know by submitting a request here: Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence (sap.com).

With the addition of SAPUI5 adaptation projects (part of SAPUI5 flexibility), SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition supports both developer extensibility (see Extending an SAP Fiori Application for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) and key user extensibility options (see Adapting SAP Fiori UIs at Runtime - Key User Adaptation).

entry.title: Learn how to work with SAPUI5 adaptation projects to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
New developer tutorials

With the addition of SAPUI5 adaptation projects (part of SAPUI5 flexibility), SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 now supports both developer extensibility and key user extensibility options.

SAPUI5 adaptation projects enable you to extend your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system by creating application variants that fulfill the specific business needs of different business roles, groups or users, organizational units, and countries. For example, you might want to create an application variant to simplify and streamline a specific process by hiding fields not required by casual users, visualizing data as a chart, or changing a responsive table to a grid table to make information easier to process.

Learn for yourself how easy it is to work with SAPUI5 adaptation projects. In these tutorials, you’ll learn step by step how to extend the Manage Credit Accounts application for Credit Controllers by creating a simplified application variant for casual users:

  1. Tutorial 1: Work with SAPUI5 adaptation projects to generate and make simple changes to an application variant of an SAP Fiori application in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (1 of 3)
  2. Tutorial 2: Work with SAPUI5 adaptation projects to extend the source code of an application variant to be deployed to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  3. Tutorial 3: Make Your Application Variant (created in an SAPUI5 adaptation project) available to business users in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

For more information, see Extending an SAP Fiori Application for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition   

Extensibility via SAPUI5 adaptation project

To check whether an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition application supports extensibility via SAPUI5 adaptation projects, look up the app in SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library. See Implementation Information under Extensibility (Extensibility via SAPUI5 Adaptation Project: Supported).

Fig 1: SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library: Implementation Information

If the SAP-delivered application you want to extend with an application variant is not yet supported, please let us know by submitting a request here: Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence (sap.com).

Let us know how you get on!

entry.title: SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402
entry.content:
Welcome to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Our fully-fledged cloud ERP is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and offers innovation and enhancements beyond measure. But even the most scalable cloud ERP would only be half as efficient without a seamless user experience. Therefore, we keep enhancing our product based on your feedback to offer you a next-level user experience so that you can thrive in today’s work environment. It is my pleasure to show you just how we do that in this release. Spoiler alert: Generative AI is involved. 😉

Check out my video below to get a first glimpse into what’s coming in the area of SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402:

Horizon Theme

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2208, we introduced the Horizon visual theme to offer you a fresh and modern SAP Fiori experience and to help you get your job done faster, more intuitively, and with better outcomes. Taking your feedback to heart, we’ve already made continuous improvements to the design in past releases. With this new release, we now implemented even more changes such as:

  • White input fields instead of gray,
  • Clearly identifiable buttons,
  • Improved contrast,
  • Reduced header size,
  • And more.

Read more about this in Thomas Reiss’s blog post.

Figure 1: The darker background on the My Home is only one of many design improvements to the Horizon visual theme.

Spaces and Pages

As announced in my previous blog post, the groups setup on the SAP Fiori launchpad is now officially outdated; spaces and pages are the only available layout option as of this release (read more in this note). Switching to spaces and pages has now become crucial to avoid a blank screen on the SAP Fiori launchpad–and note, the "switch" does not happen automatically, but has to be done manually. Read this blog post by Sylvie Charpentier on how to efficiently switch to spaces and pages. We are granting a grace period until June 2024 to all customers who haven’t switched to spaces and pages yet. During this grace period, there’s no guarantee that the group-based content is up to date. At the end of this grace period, SAP will change the settings in development and test systems first, and eventually also in production systems to display spaces and pages as default without the option for administrators and business users to switch back to groups. The administrators of affected customers will be informed about these changes in due time.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, we ship a new functionality to merge spaces and pages which can be used to e.g., reduce the number of duplicate apps, pages, or spaces for users. This can also be helpful if you want to enrich SAP-delivered spaces or pages with customer-defined content.

Figure 2: “Accounts Payable Accountant” and “Accounts Payable Manager” before merging.

Figure 3: View after merging “Accounts Payable Accountant” and “Accounts Payable Manager”.

Find out more in Kattia Jordan-Philipp's blog post.

My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Switching to spaces and pages will not only give you a new layout option to structure the access to apps following work processes in an intuitive and efficient way; it also enables you to use the My Home entry page in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Users benefit from a personalized interface tailored to their needs, with important to-dos and insights readily accessible. Designed for efficiency across devices, the My Home ensures quick task execution through intuitive app organization. With the 2402 release, we offer an increased level of personalization to make the user experience even more straightforward. This includes:

  • The harmonization of blank state,
  • Consistent group creation via drag and drop,
  • A total re-design of the app section,
  • And an increased language support for system news.

For new customers, spaces and pages and My Home are the default and you will benefit from them immediately.

Figure 4: Discover the latest enhancements to the My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402. Click on the image to watch the video.

Collaborative ERP

The My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers a new collaboration feature with Microsoft Teams. Based on contact information in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can now directly trigger a Microsoft Teams chat or voice/video call and start collaborating.

Figure 5: The integration between My Home and Microsoft Teams allows you to easily collaborate across apps. Click on the image to watch the video.

And there’s more: Another feature of the collaborative ERP is now generally available! We enable you to share your most relevant business data using cards in Microsoft Teams chats. Through the cards, recipients of the message can directly edit the data in the collaborative stage view in Microsoft Teams embedded in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Figure 6: Share business data using cards in Microsoft Teams chats and start collaborating. Click the image to watch the video.

SAP Collaboration Manager

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2302, we introduced the SAP Collaboration Manager. This cloud-based, innovative collaboration feature replaced the human-to-human chat function of the former SAP CoPilot in the three-system landscape and offers a seamlessly integrated collaboration experience directly inside the SAP Fiori launchpad, increasing overall user efficiency. Since then, we’ve been continuously enhancing the SAP Collaboration Manager to offer more capabilities such as creating chats and inviting your co-workers to collaborate without any system switch over, taking screenshots and annotating or highlighting them, and linking your chat to any screen for reference. In this release, we offer even more enhancements:

  • Add My Home “Insights” cards,
  • Mark notes and chats as “Read” or “Unread”,
  • Add notes and chats to “Favorites”.

Figure 7: The “Add Insights Cards” function allows you to provide convenient access to analytical information, lists, graphs, and associated apps.

SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects

As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, developers can work with SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio (SAP BAS) to create variants of standard applications. In an adaptation project, you can extend the original code to define changes that fulfill the specific business requirements of different business roles, user groups, organizational units, and countries. For example, you might want to simplify and streamline a specific process by hiding fields not required by casual users, visualizing data as a chart, or changing a responsive table to a grid table to make information easier to process. After deployment, both the original application and your adapted application exist in parallel and can be made available to the relevant users through uniquely name application tiles in SAP Fiori launchpad/My Home.

Figure 8: You can now create SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio to generate app variants. Click the image to watch the video.

Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402

In my first paragraph of this blog post, I promised to talk about generative AI–so, let’s talk about SAP’s brand-new, natural language genAI copilot, Joule. Joule is a comprehensive solution designed to understand the complexities of your business processes and will completely revolutionize how you interact with the system. It provides transparent, role-based access to relevant processes, making interactions intuitive and seamless. By enabling natural conversations between users and systems, Joule enhances productivity and delivers valuable insights. The three supported conversational patterns are:

  • Navigational: Joule helps users find required functionalities within SAP systems,
  • Transactional: Joule assists users in completing tasks,
  • Informational: Joule provides insights based on SAP Help documentation data.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, Joule is taking off through an Early Adopter Care program and will be available for selected customers for initial testing. Read more about Joule and other AI innovations in this blog post by Syed Talal Hussain.

Figure 9: SAP’s generative AI copilot Joule revolutionizes how you interact with the system. Click the image to watch the video.

Customer Influence Page

Our top priority has always been ensuring a seamless and straightforward user experience. Reacting to your feedback and requirements is the key to achieving this. The main input channel where we receive your requirements is the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, User Experience and Cross-Topics customer influence page. To make sure we collect all your requests, we emphasize the importance of the channel and increase the threshold to 10 to get a better focus. We review permanently and stop all improvement requests that are older than 18 months and have not (yet) met the current voting threshold. Based on UX importance, here is a status report of features that were delivered and that are still in process/planning stage:

  • S4HC Home page personalization: User CB ID to be shown under “User Account” menu (19 votes), 2nd most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Delivered with current release
  • Adapt UI – Enable ability to set field properties (i.e., mandatory, visible) (15 votes), 4th most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Can be achieved through UI Adaptation Projects
  • Upload custom logo without having to use UI Theme Designer (2TV) (13 votes), 6th most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Planned with next release (Q3/2024)
  • Enable a download/upload of the My Home app tile information (11 votes), 11th  most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Delivered with current release

Figure 10: Our customer influence page is the main channel to request requirements.

When I said we are offering next-level user experience with this new release, I wasn’t promising too much. Get more details about the innovations and enhancements mentioned in this blog post and much, much more in Thomas Reiss’s blog post.

Feel free to drop a kudo if you liked this blog post. Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our new community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. Looking forward to the next release!

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

📽️ Watch Our Live Sessions

In January 2024, we hosted a series of 19 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Missed the live sessions? No problem! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you on demand.

 

🎙️ Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Subscribe now and benefit from the shared knowledge!

 

💻openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Our openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud offer an exciting new learning opportunity. What began with a small batch of 20 videos, has now become a channel with more than 50 microlearnings that have generated over 20,000 views. Today, we cover multiple lines of business such as finance, manufacturing, and supply chain management, and key technology topics like Master Data Management, extensibility, SAP User Experience, and upgrade management. We are continuously adding new microlearnings to our channel, so make sure you check them out.

 

🗣️ Your Voice Matters

You want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics? We have just the right place for you! Join our interactive community where we bring together customers, partners, and SAP experts with a clear mission: To engage with one another about best practices and product solutions. We invite you to explore the ‘one-stop shop’ as the central place for all resources, tools, content questions, answers and to connect with experts who will guide you through your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise.

 

🔎Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP.
